---
title: 'Socio Vulnerability Index'
author: 'Cassie'
format: html
---

0. Loading
```{python}
import pandas as pd
from pathlib import Path
import os
import geopandas as gpd
from shapely.geometry import Point
import numpy as np

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
```

# For SocioEconomic Vulnerability Index
1. Reading in all the file
```{python}
DATA_CLEAN_DIR = Path("data/derived-data")
DATA_DIR = Path("data/raw-data")

income = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "income_clean.csv")
renter = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "renter_clean.csv")
housing_age = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "housing_age_clean.csv")
home_value = pd.read_csv(DATA_CLEAN_DIR / "home_value_clean.csv")

tracts = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / 'tl_2023_17_tract'/ "tl_2023_17_tract.shp")

```

2. Clip All Dataframe into Chicago Shape
```{python}
tracts = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / 'tl_2023_17_tract'/ "tl_2023_17_tract.shp")
tracts["GEOID"] = tracts["GEOID"].astype(str)
cook_tracts = tracts[tracts["COUNTYFP"] == "031"].copy()

def make_it_geo(df, base_map):
    df["GEOID"] = df["GEOID"].astype(str)
    return base_map.merge(df, on="GEOID", how="inner")

gdf_income = make_it_geo(income, cook_tracts)
gdf_renter = make_it_geo(renter, cook_tracts)
gdf_age = make_it_geo(housing_age, cook_tracts)
gdf_home = make_it_geo(home_value, cook_tracts)

city = gpd.read_file(DATA_DIR / "Boundaries_Chicago.geojson")
city = city.to_crs(gdf_income.crs)
city_union = city.geometry.unary_union

def clip_to_chicago_and_save(gdf, name):
    gdf = gdf.to_crs(city.crs)
    
    gdf_chi = gdf[gdf.geometry.centroid.within(city_union)].copy()
    
    # as csv
    cols_to_save = [c for c in gdf_chi.columns if c != 'geometry']
    csv_filename = f"{name}_chicago.csv"
    gdf_chi[cols_to_save].to_csv(csv_filename, index=False)
    
    # as gpkg
    gdf_chi.crs = "EPSG:4326" 
    gpkg_filename = f"{name}_chicago.gpkg"
    gdf_chi.to_file(gpkg_filename, layer='tracts', driver="GPKG")
    
    print(f"✅ {name} done:")
    print(f"   - Number of Tracts being kept: {len(gdf_chi)}")
    print(f"   - Saved: {csv_filename} & {gpkg_filename}")
    return gdf_chi

gdfs = {"income_24c": gdf_income, "renter_24c": gdf_renter, "house_age_24c": gdf_age, "home_value_24c": gdf_home}
chicago_gdfs = {} 

for name, target_gdf in gdfs.items():
    chicago_gdfs[name] = clip_to_chicago_and_save(target_gdf, name)

income_chi = chicago_gdfs["income_24c"]
renter_chi = chicago_gdfs["renter_24c"]
age_chi = chicago_gdfs["house_age_24c"]
home_value_chi = chicago_gdfs["home_value_24c"]

```

3. Standardizing all variables
```{python}
def zscore(series):
    return (series - series.mean()) / series.std()

income_chi['income_z'] = -zscore(income_chi['median_income'])
renter_chi['renter_z'] = zscore(renter_chi['renter_share'])
age_chi['age_z'] = zscore(age_chi['old_housing_share'])
home_value_chi['home_value_z'] = -zscore(home_value_chi['median_home_value'])

```

4. Sanity Check for each variable
```{python}
print(income_chi['income_z'].describe())
print(renter_chi['renter_z'].describe())
print(age_chi['age_z'].describe())
print(home_value_chi['home_value_z'].describe())
```

5. Setting up the Socio Index
```{python}
socio = income_chi[['GEOID', 'geometry', 'income_z']].merge(
    renter_chi[['GEOID', 'renter_z']],
    on='GEOID', how = 'inner').merge(
    age_chi[['GEOID', 'age_z']],
    on='GEOID', how = 'inner').merge(
    home_value_chi[['GEOID', 'home_value_z']],
    on='GEOID', how = 'inner')

socio['socio_index'] = (
    2 * socio['income_z'] +
    0.5 * socio['renter_z'] +
    0.5 * socio['age_z'] +
    2 * socio['home_value_z']) / 5

socio['socio_index'].describe()
```

6. Mapping socio vulnerability index
```{python}
max_abs = abs(socio['socio_index']).max()
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6,8))

socio.plot(
    column='socio_index',
    cmap='RdBu_r',
    vmin=-2,
    vmax=2,
    legend=True,
    ax=ax
)

ax.set_title("Socioeconomic Vulnerability Index (Chicago)")
ax.set_axis_off()
plt.show()
```
